description:
If you’re still figuring out which term insurance is right for you, book a FREE call with Ditto. We’ll help you compare different plans and pick what’s best for your life and wallet. Just two minutes to book: https://ditto.sh/vnpe3r

ICICI Prudential iProtect Smart: https://joinditto.in/term-insurance/icici-prudential/iprotect-smart/

ICICI Prudential is one of the biggest names in life insurance in India. As a joint venture between ICICI Bank and Prudential UK, it’s been around since 2000, managing ₹19,774 crores in business with a claim settlement ratio of 98.03 percent. That alone makes ICICI Life Insurance a strong contender for your term life insurance needs.

So what makes ICICI Prudential iProtect Smart Plus stand out? This ICICI term plan offers flexibility with payment modes—monthly, half-yearly, yearly—and multiple premium payment terms, from single pay to pay till 60 or regular pay. It suits both salaried and self-employed profiles depending on underwriting. The ICICI Prudential term insurance plan comes with smart benefit options: Life, Life Plus, and Life Rebalancing. Each variant tweaks how the death benefit and accidental benefit are structured.

Key highlights? The ICICI Prudential iProtect Smart Plus plan includes a terminal illness benefit that pays 100 percent of the sum assured if two doctors certify the insured has less than six months to live. You also get a Life Stage Protection option that lets you increase your ICICI Prudential term cover after marriage, childbirth, or taking a home loan. This flexibility is unmatched and makes ICICI term insurance truly adaptive to life changes.

Midway through? Not sure if ICICI Prudential is right for your life insurance goals? Talk to Ditto’s expert advisors. They’ll help you compare ICICI Prudential iProtect Smart Plus with HDFC Life Click2Protect, Bajaj Allianz Life eTouch II, Axis Max Life Smart Term Plan Plus, and Tata AIA Sampoorna Raksha Promise—side by side. It’s free, no pressure, just clarity. Book here: https://ditto.sh/rcxbg5

The ICICI Prudential iProtect Smart Plus plan also introduces Premium Breaks—allowing you to pause premiums for a year after five years, with a minimum five-year gap in between breaks. Their Smart Exit feature refunds your premiums if you exit after 25 years and are over 60. Plus, there’s an Insta Payment on claim intimation: if your sum assured is ₹1 crore or more, the nominee gets ₹3 lakh within one working day to manage immediate expenses.

Looking at the ICICI Prudential term insurance riders? You have a Critical Illness Rider covering up to 60 diseases (much broader than its older version’s 34 illnesses), and an Accidental Death Benefit rider. While accidental death is already covered in the base ICICI Life Insurance term plan, the rider pays an extra lump sum for accident cases. For most, adding the Critical Illness Rider is a smart move—it acts as an income replacement if a serious disease strikes, without eating into your base term insurance cover.

Where does ICICI Prudential iProtect Smart Plus lag? It has a slightly higher complaint volume (11 per 10,000 claims) compared to HDFC or Axis, and while ICICI Life Insurance’s operational metrics are solid, they’re not yet at HDFC’s scale. But for most people seeking a feature-rich term life insurance plan from a reliable brand, ICICI Prudential term insurance remains an excellent choice.

0:00 – Introduction to ICICI Prudential iProtect Smart Plus 
0:52 – About ICICI Prudential

Still confused? Want to explore ICICI Prudential iProtect Smart Plus alongside other plans? Just book a free call with Ditto. We’ll break down your options in simple English, run actual premium quotes, and even help during the claim stage years later. No spam, no hidden agenda—just honest advice. https://ditto.sh/vnpe3r

Happy Insurance Hunting!

comments.text:
I’m extremely disappointed with the experience I’ve had with ICICI Prudential. Despite paying my premiums diligently for the past 6 years, the company is now asking for a long list of unnecessary and intrusive documents — including my last 3 years’ income proof, ITRs, personal documents, and even details of policies I hold with other companies.

This is highly frustrating and seems like a deliberate attempt to delay or increase the premium under the guise of formalities. I’m 100% healthy and fit — a fact confirmed by the medical test they themselves conducted.

Still, they are refusing to reinstate my policy even after submitting everything they asked for. This raises serious concerns about how difficult it might be if someone files a claim in the future.

This kind of poor service and lack of transparency is unacceptable from a company that claims to care for its customers. I would strongly advise others to reconsider before trusting ICICI Prudential with your insurance needs.
